source_book: The 4 Disciplines of Execution (2nd ed.) — McChesney, Covey, Huling, Thele, Walker
_comment: Test prompts in EN/JP/zh-TW (skill description is multilingual). Field names + types in English. Minimum 6: ≥3 should_trigger, ≥2 should_not_trigger (lures), ≥1 edge_case.

test_cases.prompt: I have a goal but don't know what daily action drives it.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery; gate on WIG, then run Socratic two-axis discovery (predictive AND influenceable) to land 2-3 lead measures.
test_cases.notes: Canonical EN trigger — user has lag, lacks lead. Direct match for description's 'I have the goal, what do I DO?' phrasing.

test_cases.prompt: 目標はあるが日々何をすれば結果に繋がるか分からない。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery; WIG ゲート確認後、二軸テスト(予測性 × 影響可能性)で先行指標 2-3 個を抽出する。
test_cases.notes: Canonical JP trigger — direct match for description's 「目標はあるが、毎日何をすれば結果に繋がるのか分からない」.

test_cases.prompt: 我有 WIG 了，但不知道每天該做什麼。
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery；先確認 WIG 一句話可說，再以兩軸測試(預測性 × 可影響性)挑出 2-3 個 lead measures。
test_cases.notes: Canonical zh-TW trigger — user explicitly mentions WIG (4DX vocabulary), so D1 redirect not needed; this is exactly D2 territory.

test_cases.prompt: I have too many priorities and can't decide what to focus on.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery. This is pre-WIG: user hasn't yet narrowed to a single Wildly Important Goal. Should fire 4dx-d1-personal-wig-defining instead.
test_cases.notes: lure: pre-WIG state. Description explicitly says 'Do NOT activate for: defining the goal itself (use 4dx-d1-personal-wig-defining)'.

test_cases.prompt: Design KPIs for our marketing team.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery. This is generic enterprise BI / KPI design — not personal lead-measure discovery, no defined personal WIG, and 'team KPI design' lacks the daily-influenceable constraint that defines a lead measure.
test_cases.notes: lure: generic KPI design. Surface vocabulary (KPI, metrics) overlaps but lacks the WIG + personal-influenceable axes.

test_cases.prompt: I want to track my habits in Notion — what's the best template?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery. Habit-tracker tooling request, not lead-measure logic. Description warns: 'a habit is a behavior; a lead measure is a measurement of a behavior tied to a specific WIG.' No WIG mentioned, no causal-chain articulation requested.
test_cases.notes: lure: habit-tracker tooling. Surface similarity (daily behavior tracking) but missing the predictive-tied-to-lag requirement.

test_cases.prompt: How do I track my weekly weight loss?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Do NOT activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery. Weekly weight is the lag-on-a-shorter-clock (CE-01 lag-masquerading-as-lead), not a lead measure. If anything, redirect to 4dx-d3-personal-scoreboard for display, but the user is asking about lag tracking, not lead discovery.
test_cases.notes: lure: lag-on-shorter-clock confusion. Tests that the skill resists firing on tracking-the-lag requests.

test_cases.prompt: Can stock-market direction be a lead measure for my portfolio P&L?
test_cases.expected_behavior: Activate 4dx-d2-personal-lead-measure-discovery AND apply the two-axis test loudly: stock-market direction is predictive (axis 1 ✓) but not influenceable (axis 2 ✗) — disqualified. This is the canonical CE-02 'predictive but not influenceable' failure mode (rainfall analogy). Skill should fire precisely BECAUSE the user is about to make this mistake.
test_cases.notes: boundary: predictive-but-not-influenceable. The user is implicitly asking for lead-measure validation; firing the skill is correct because its core job is to surface the two-axis test on exactly this kind of candidate. Description explicitly says 'surface the two-axis test loudly' — this is its raison d'être.

ship_threshold_note: 100% to ship cleanly. ≥80% allowed only with explicit failure analysis. <80% must redo Stage 2 A2/E/B. should_not_trigger has zero tolerance.
